properties.name: Watch & Act
properties.webBody:
This Major Flood Warning is being issued for the King River . 

RIVER LEVELS PEAKING JUST ABOVE THE MAJOR FLOOD LEVEL AT DOCKER RD BRIDGE

Moderate rainfall since Monday has caused significant river level rises and areas of flooding across the Ovens and King River catchments. Showers are forecast to continue through Friday, then ease over theweekend. 

River levels along the King River at Docker Road Bridge are currently peaking just above the major flood level.

Moderate flooding is occurring along the Ovens River at Wangaratta. 

Minor flooding is occurring along the Buffalo River downstream of Lake Buffalo, along the King River downstream of Lake William Hovell and along the Ovens River at Bright, Eurobin and Rocky Point.

King River:
River levels are peaking just above the major flood level at Docker Rd Bridge. 

Minor flooding is occurringdownstream of Lake William Hovell and Cheshunt. The King River at Lake William Hovell (Downstream) is currently at 2.17m and falling, with minor flooding. The King River at Lake William Hovell (Downstream) is likely to remain above the minor flood level (1.80m)overnight Friday into Saturday.

The King River at Docker Rd Bridge is currently at 4.14 m and peaking just above the major flood level . 

The King River at Docker Rd Bridge is likely to remain above the major flood level (4.10 m) overnight Friday into Saturday. The river may remain above the moderate flood level (3.95 m) into Saturday morning.
Stay safe - avoid the flooded area. Be aware of potential hazards caused by flooding and never enter floodwater.
What you should do:
  • Floodwater is dangerous - never enter floodwater.
If you choose to leave:
  • Remember to take your pets, mobile phone, spare clothes and medications.
  • Travel to the home of family or friends who are in a safe location, away from flooding.
  • Turn off gas and electricity at your home or workplace.
  • Be aware of any road closures when you leave.
If you are travelling:
  • Do not enter flooded areas. Turn around a find a safe alternative route.
  • Be aware of road hazards including mud, debris and damaged roads or bridges.
  • Floodwater is dangerous - never drive, walk or ride through floodwater.
  • Multiple road closures are in effect around Wangaratta - Check the VicTraffic website (traffic.transport.vic.gov.au) before you travel
If you stay or if it is unsafe to leave:
  • Make sure you have enough food, drinking water, medications and pet food to survive for three to five days in case you become isolated.
  • Use plugs, sandbags or household items to block toilets, sinks and drains to stop sewerage backflow.
  • Lift it - move valuables and vital household items up on high surfaces, such as on top of tables, chairs, benches and beds.

Impacts in your area:

Impacts in your area:
  • Main roads and bridges may be closed due to flooding.
  • Campers at Edi Cutting have been moved on to higher ground. 
  • Boating in floodwaters is dangerous due to debris, submerged trees and other hidden dangers. 
  • Check the VicTraffic website (www.traffic.transport.vic.gov.au) for road closures and follow instructions from emergency services.
This message was issued by State Emergency Service.

The next update is expected by 04/07/2026 11:00 am or as the situation changes.

Use multiple sources to stay informed:
  • www.emergency.vic.gov.au
  • VicEmergency Hotline - freecall 1800 226 226
  • VicEmergency app
  • Facebook or Twitter (#vicfloods)
  • Tune in to ABC Local Radio, commercial and designated community radio stations, or Sky News TV.
Accessibility:
The following services can help you, or someone you know, access information during an emergency.
  • To access this information in other languages, call the Translating and Interpreting Service on 131 450 (freecall) and ask them to call VicEmergency Hotline.
  • If you are deaf, hard of hearing, or have a speech/communication impairment contact the National Relay Service and ask them to call the VicEmergency Hotline.

properties.name: Advice
properties.webBody:
This Minor Flood Warning is being issued for the Mitchell River to Glenaladale.

Minor flooding is occurring at Waterford and at Glenaladale

Moderate to heavy rainfall has been recorded across the Mitchell River catchment over the past few days,resulting in river level rises. 
Minor flooding is occurring along the Mitchell River at Glenaladale.

Mitchell River to Glenaladale:

Minor flooding is occurring along the Mitchell River at Glenaladale. 

The Mitchell River at Glenaladale is currently at 3.81 metres and rising, with minor flooding. 
The Mitchell River at Glenaladale may peak near 4.00 metres early Saturday morning, with minor flooding. 
The river may remain above the minor flood level (3.00 metres) during Saturday.
Stay informed - monitor your local conditions and remain alert.
What you should do: 
  • Be prepared to act if your situation changes.
  • Stay informed by listening to emergency broadcasters and monitoring warnings.
  • Floodwater is dangerous - never drive, walk or ride through floodwater.
  • Farmers should consider moving livestock and machinery to higher ground.
Impacts in your area: 
  • High river levels and potential flooding in areas close to rivers and streams.
  • Bairnsdale-Dargo Road, Lindenow-Glenaladale Road and Rivermouth Roadmay be closed and low bridges may be underwater.
  • Stagnant water can be harmful to your health. Don’t swim, drink or wash in stagnant water.
  • Boating in floodwaters is dangerous due to debris, submerged trees and other hidden dangers. 
This message was issued by State Emergency Service.

The next update is expected by 04/07/2026 02:00 pm or as the situation changes.

Use multiple sources to stay informed:
  • www.emergency.vic.gov.au
  • VicEmergency Hotline - freecall 1800 226 226
  • VicEmergency app
  • Facebook or Twitter (#vicfloods)
  • Tune in to ABC Local Radio, commercial and designated community radio stations, or Sky News TV.
Accessibility: 
The following services can help you, or someone you know, access information during an emergency. 
  • To access this information in other languages call the Translating and Interpreting Service on 131 450 (freecall) and ask them to call VicEmergency Hotline. 
  • If you are deaf, hard of hearing, or have a speech/communication impairment contact the National Relay Service and ask them to call the VicEmergency Hotline.

properties.name: Watch & Act
properties.location: the Ovens River downstream of Rocky Point
properties.webBody:
This Moderate Flood Warning is being issued for the Ovens River downstream of Rocky Point.

MODERATE FLOODING OCCURRING AT WANGARATTA

Moderate rainfall since Monday has caused significant river level rises and areas of flooding across theOvens and King River catchments. Showers are forecast to continue through Friday, then ease over theweekend. 

River levels along the King River at Docker Road Bridge are currently peaking just above the major flood level. 

Moderate flooding is occurring along the Ovens River at Wangaratta.Minor flooding is occurring along the Buffalo River downstream of Lake Buffalo, along the King Riverdownstream of Lake William Hovell and along the Ovens River at Bright, Eurobin and Rocky Point.

Ovens River downstream of Rocky Point:

Moderate flooding is occurring at Wangaratta.

The Ovens River at Wangaratta is currently at 12.55 m and rising, above the moderate flood level (12.40 m). The Ovens River at Wangaratta may reach around 12.60 m Saturday morning, with moderate flooding.
Stay safe - avoid the flooded area. People in riverside properties should monitor river levels closely overnight and follow their emergency plan.

What you should do:
  • People who live or work along the Ovens River should monitor river levels closely overnight and follow their emergency plan, including people who live within the Parfitt Road and Wilson Road levees. 
  • Locate your emergency kit, if you have one. 
  • Floodwater is dangerous - never enter floodwater.
If you choose to leave:
  • Remember to take your pets, mobile phone, spare clothes and medications.
  • Travel to the home of family or friends who are in a safe location, away from flooding.
  • Turn off gas and electricity at your home or workplace.
  • Be aware of any road closures when you leave.
If you are travelling:
  • Do not enter flooded areas. Turn around a find a safe alternative route.
  • Be aware of road hazards including mud, debris and damaged roads or bridges.
  • Floodwater is dangerous - never drive, walk or ride through floodwater.
If you stay or if it is unsafe to leave:
  • Make sure you have enough food, drinking water, medications and pet food to survive for three to five days in case you become isolated.
  • Use plugs, sandbags or household items to block toilets, sinks and drains to stop sewerage backflow.
  • Lift it - move valuables and vital household items up on high surfaces, such as on top of tables, chairs, benches and beds.

Impacts in your area:

Impacts in your area - Wangaratta: 
  • At the predicted 12.6 metres, floodwater may reach between Yogi's mouth and nose in APEX park, and floodwater from the King River may fill the Kaluna Wetlands into Merriwa Park. 
  • Deep floodwater around Northern Beaches, Sydney Beach, Pinkerton Crescent. 
  • Bickerton Street and Mundgee Place carparks are flooded, and Painters Island Caravan Park is now closed. 
  • Moderate flooding of Pinkerton Crescent.
  • Floodwater will enter backyards along Templeton Street and Baker Street. 
  • Many riverside and creekside paths and bike tracks are now closed.
  • There will be extensive local road closures - drive with care and never enter floodwater. 
  • Floodplains and farmland to the North, South and East of Wangaratta have started to flood around Tarrawingee, Markwood, Oxley Flats, Londrigan, Bowser, Docker Plains and North Wangaratta. 
  • Check the Wangaratta Local Flood Guide for more local impacts and advice https://www.ses.vic.gov.au/plan-and-stay-safe/flood-guides/rural-city-of-wangaratta#wangaratta-local-flood-guide
Impacts in your area - Whorouly: 
  • Widespread farmland with minor flooding. 
  • The Snow Road may be closed between the Great Alpine Road and Carboor-Everton Road. 
  • The Whorouly Road North of the shop may be closed to the Snow Road. 
Road Closures:
  • Great Alpine Road at Yellow Creek (Detour Road bypass is in place) For more information on road closures go to www.traffic.transport.vic.gov.au.
This message was issued by State Emergency Service.

The next update is expected by 04/07/2026 11:00 am or as the situation changes.

Use multiple sources to stay informed:
  • www.emergency.vic.gov.au
  • VicEmergency Hotline - freecall 1800 226 226
  • VicEmergency app
  • Facebook or Twitter (#vicfloods)
  • Tune in to ABC Local Radio, commercial and designated community radio stations, or Sky News TV.
Accessibility:
The following services can help you, or someone you know, access information during an emergency.
  • To access this information in other languages, call the Translating and Interpreting Service on 131 450 (freecall) and ask them to call VicEmergency Hotline.
  • If you are deaf, hard of hearing, or have a speech/communication impairment contact the National Relay Service and ask them to call the VicEmergency Hotline.

properties.webBody:
This Minor Flood Warning is being issued for Murray River to Biggara.

MINOR FLOODING OCCURRING AT BIGGARA AND POSSIBLE AT BRINGENBRONG

Moderate to heavy rainfall has been recorded across the Upper Murray River catchment over the past few days, resulting in river level rises. 

Minor flooding is occurring along the Murray River at Biggara and is possible at Bringenbrong from early Saturday morning.

Murray River to Biggara:
Minor flooding is occurring at Biggara.
Stay informed - monitor your local conditions and remain alert.
What you should do: 
  • Floodwater is dangerous - never drive, walk or ride through floodwater.
  • Be prepared to act if your situation changes.
  • Stay informed by listening to emergency broadcasters and monitoring warnings.
  • Farmers should consider moving livestock and machinery to higher ground.
  • Monitor weather forecasts and river levels. Go to www.bom.gov.au/vic/warnings/
Impacts in your area: 
  • High river levels and potential flooding in areas close to rivers and streams.
  • Local roads may be closed and low bridges may be underwater.
  • Heavy rainfall may lead to an increased risk of landslides – be aware of fallen earth, rocks, debris or trees.
  • Caravan parks and camping grounds may be flooded.
This message was issued by State Emergency Service.

The next update is expected by 04/07/2026 02:30 pm or as the situation changes.

Use multiple sources to stay informed:
  • www.emergency.vic.gov.au
  • VicEmergency Hotline - freecall 1800 226 226
  • VicEmergency app
  • Facebook or Twitter (#vicfloods)
  • Tune in to ABC Local Radio, commercial and designated community radio stations, or Sky News TV.
Accessibility: 
The following services can help you, or someone you know, access information during an emergency. 
  • To access this information in other languages call the Translating and Interpreting Service on 131 450 (freecall) and ask them to call VicEmergency Hotline. 
  • If you are deaf, hard of hearing, or have a speech/communication impairment contact the National Relay Service and ask them to call the VicEmergency Hotline.

This Minor Flood Warning is being issued for the Kiewa River to Mongans Bridge.

MINOR FLOODING OCCURRING AT MONGANS BRIDGE AND KIEWA

Moderate to heavy rainfall across the Kiewa River catchment over the past few days has resulted insignificant river level rises along the Kiewa River. 

Minor flooding is occurring at Mongans Bridge and Kiewa, and moderate flooding is likely at Bandiana from Friday evening

Kiewa River to Mongans Bridge:

Minor flooding is occurring at Mongans Bridge.
Stay informed - monitor your local conditions and remain alert.
What you should do: 
  • Be prepared to act if your situation changes.

  • Floodwater is dangerous - never drive, walk or ride through floodwater.
  • Farmers should consider moving livestock and machinery to higher ground.
  • Floodwater is toxic - never play or swim in floodwater.
Impacts in your area:

  • Local roads may be closed and low bridges may be underwater.

properties.name: Advice
properties.webBody:
This is a Final Minor Flood Warning for Werribee River to Melton Reservoir. 
  • Flooding has eased at Ballan.
  • Flooding has eased along the Upper Werribee River at Ballan after a minor flood peak Friday morning.
  • The Werribee River at Ballan peaked at 2.03 m around 08:30 am Friday, with minor flooding. 
  • The river is currently at 1.54 m and falling, below the minor level. 
  • The Werribee River at Ballan is expected to remain below the minor flood level (1.60 m).
The threat of flooding is reduced. Stay informed - monitor your local conditions and beware of hazards remaining in the warning area.
What you should do:
  • Be prepared to act if your situation changes.
  • Floodwater is dangerous - never enter floodwater.
If your home or business has been flooded:
  • Check for building damage before you enter.
  • Have all electrical and gas equipment professionally tested before use.
  • Clean and dry out the building as soon as possible.
Call Triple Zero (000) in a life threatening emergency. For emergency assistance from the SES call 132 500.
Impacts in your area: 
  • Damage to roads and buildings caused by flooding may still exist in your area. 
This message was issued by State Emergency Service.

This will be the last warning for this flood. 

Use multiple sources to stay informed:
  • www.emergency.vic.gov.au
  • VicEmergency Hotline - freecall 1800 226 226
  • VicEmergency app
  • Facebook or Twitter (#vicfloods)
  • Tune in to ABC Local Radio, commercial and designated community radio stations, or Sky News TV.
Accessibility:
The following services can help you, or someone you know, access information during an emergency.
  • To access this information in other languages call the Translating and Interpreting Service on 131 450 (freecall) and ask them to call VicEmergency Hotline.
  • If you are deaf, hard of hearing, or have a speech/communication impairment contact the National Relay Service and ask them to call the VicEmergency Hotline.

MINOR FLOODING OCCURRING AT BRIGHT, EUROBIN, ROCKY POINT, DOWNSTREAM OF LAKE BUFFALO AND DOWNSTREAM OF LAKE WILLIAM HOVELL

Moderate rainfall since Monday has caused significant river level rises and areas of flooding across the Ovens and King River catchments. Showers are forecast to continue through Friday, then ease over the weekend. 

River levels along the King River at Docker Road Bridge are currently peaking just above the major flood level.

Moderate flooding is occurring along the Ovens River at Wangaratta. 

Minor flooding is occurring along the Buffalo River downstream of Lake Buffalo, along the King Riverdownstream of Lake William Hovell and along the Ovens River at Bright, Eurobin and Rocky Point.

Buffalo River:
Minor flooding is occurring downstream of Lake Buffalo.

The Buffalo River at Lake Buffalo (Downstream) peaked at 5.31 m around 10:45 am Friday 3 July, withmoderate flooding. The river is currently at 4.95 m and falling, with minor flooding. The Buffalo River at Lake Buffalo (Downstream) is likely to remain above the minor flood level (3.20 m) during Saturday.
Stay informed - monitor your local conditions and remain alert.

properties.name: Advice
properties.location: the Seven Creeks downstream of Euroa
properties.webBody:
This Minor Flood Warning is being issued for Seven Creeks downstream of Euroa.

MINOR FLOODING OCCURRING AT EUROA AND POSSIBLE AT KIALLA WEST FROM SATURDAY MORNING

Since Monday, moderate rainfall has been recorded across the Seven and Castle Creeks catchment, resulting in creek level rises. Minor flooding is occurring along the Seven Creeks at Strathbogie and Euroa. Downstream at Kialla West minor flooding is possible from Saturday morning.

Seven Creeks downstream of Euroa:

Minor flooding is possible at Kialla West. 

The Seven Creeks at Kialla West is currently at 4.01 m and steady, below the minor flood level. The Seven Creeks at Kialla West may reach around the minor flood level (4.50 m) from Saturday morning.
Stay informed - monitor your local conditions and remain alert.
What you should do: 
  • Floodwater is dangerous - never drive, walk or ride through floodwater.
  • Be prepared to act if your situation changes.
  • Stay informed by listening to emergency broadcasters and monitoring warnings. 
  • Farmers should consider moving livestock and machinery to higher ground.
  • Download Shepparton Mooroopna and Kialla Local Flood Guide to understand local flood impacts. 
  • Review your emergency plan and check your emergency kit is fully stocked, if you have one.
  • Monitor weather forecasts and river levels. Go to www.bom.gov.au/vic/warnings/
Impacts in your area: 
  • High river levels and potential flooding in areas close to rivers and streams.
  • Local roads may be closed and low bridges may be underwater.
  • If Minor flooding does happen in Kialla West:  Mitchell Road may flood between the Goulburn Valley Highway and Archer Street.    
This message was issued by State Emergency Service.

The next update is expected by 04/07/2026 03:00 pm or as the situation changes.

Use multiple sources to stay informed:
  • www.emergency.vic.gov.au
  • VicEmergency Hotline - freecall 1800 226 226
  • VicEmergency app
  • Facebook or Twitter (#vicfloods)
  • Tune in to ABC Local Radio, commercial and designated community radio stations, or Sky News TV.
Accessibility: 
The following services can help you, or someone you know, access information during an emergency. 
  • To access this information in other languages call the Translating and Interpreting Service on 131 450 (freecall) and ask them to call VicEmergency Hotline. 
  • If you are deaf, hard of hearing, or have a speech/communication impairment contact the National Relay Service and ask them to call the VicEmergency Hotline.

Incident Location: the Seven Creeks downstream of Euroa

Seven Creeks downstream of Euroa:

Minor flooding is possible at Kialla West. 

The Seven Creeks at Kialla West is currently at 4.01 m and steady, below the minor flood level. The Seven Creeks at Kialla West may reach around the minor flood level (4.50 m) from Saturday morning. 

Stay informed - monitor your local conditions and remain alert.

properties.name: Advice
properties.location: the Goulburn River Seymour to Shepparton
properties.webBody:
This Minor Flood Warning is being issued for the Goulburn River Seymour to Shepparton.

Minor flooding is possible at Murchison and Shepparton from early Saturday morning. 

Moderate rainfall since Monday has caused river and creek level rises in the Goulburn River catchment, where minor flooding has eased at Taggerty and minor flooding is occurring at Seymour. Minor flooding may extend downstream to Murchison and Shepparton from early Saturday morning.

The Goulburn River at Murchison is currently at 7.44 metres and rising, and may exceed the minor flood level of 9.00 metres early Saturday morning. A peak height of 9.10 metres is expected Saturday morning, with minor flooding.

The Goulburn River at Shepparton is currently at 8.31 metres and rising, and may exceed the minor flood level of 9.50 metres Saturday morning. 

Further rises are possible as upstream flows arrive.
Stay informed - monitor your local conditions and remain alert.
What you should do: 
  • Farmers should consider moving livestock and machinery to higher ground.
  • Floodwater is dangerous - never drive, walk or ride through floodwater.
  • Be prepared to act if your situation changes.
  • Stay informed by listening to emergency broadcasters and monitoring warnings.
  • Monitor weather forecasts and river levels. Go to www.bom.gov.au/vic/warnings/
Impacts in your area: 
  • As floodwater moves along the Goulburn River from the Seymour area, and further rainfall is forecast during Friday into Saturday, river levels in the Goulburn River will start to rise.
For Murchison:
  • Floodwater on low-lying rural properties upstream and downstream of Murchison
  • Floodwater may flow through the Police Paddocks and reach the end of Watson Street near Charles Lane
  • Floodwater may reach the caravan area behind the ovals at the recreation reserve on River Road. 
For Toolamba:
  • Tracks in the forested areas west of Goulburn River, such as Steves Track are likely to be flooded by Saturday morning. 
For Kialla:
  • Low-lying local roads, including Watt & Raftery Roads at Kialla, may start to flood and low bridges may be underwater.
  • Due to flooding on the Seven Creeks, Mitchell Road may flood between the Goulburn Valley Highway and Archer Street.    
  • Parks, tracks and campgrounds may be closed due to flooding.
This message was issued by State Emergency Service.

The next update is expected by 04/07/2026 12:00 pm or as the situation changes.

Use multiple sources to stay informed:
  • www.emergency.vic.gov.au
  • VicEmergency Hotline - freecall 1800 226 226
  • VicEmergency app
  • Facebook or Twitter (#vicfloods)
  • Tune in to ABC Local Radio, commercial and designated community radio stations, or Sky News TV.
Accessibility: 
The following services can help you, or someone you know, access information during an emergency. 
  • To access this information in other languages call the Translating and Interpreting Service on 131 450 (freecall) and ask them to call VicEmergency Hotline. 
  • If you are deaf, hard of hearing, or have a speech/communication impairment contact the National Relay Service and ask them to call the VicEmergency Hotline.

properties.location: the Ovens River Bright to Rocky Point
properties.webBody:
This Minor Flood Warning is being issued for the Ovens River Bright to Rocky Point.

MINOR FLOODING OCCURRING AT BRIGHT, EUROBIN, ROCKY POINT, DOWNSTREAM OF LAKE BUFFALO AND DOWNSTREAM OF LAKE WILLIAM HOVELL

Moderate rainfall since Monday has caused significant river level rises and areas of flooding across the Ovens and King River catchments. Showers are forecast to continue through Friday, then ease over the weekend. 

River levels along the King River at Docker Road Bridge are currently peaking just above the major flood level.

Moderate flooding is occurring along the Ovens River at Wangaratta.

Minor flooding is occurring along the Buffalo River downstream of Lake Buffalo, along the King Riverdownstream of Lake William Hovell and along the Ovens River at Bright, Eurobin and Rocky Point.

Ovens River Bright to Rocky Point:

Minor flooding is occurring at Eurobin and Rocky Point.The Ovens River at Eurobin peaked at 4.94 m around 03:00 pm Friday 03 July, with minor flooding. The river is currently at 4.86 m and falling, with minor flooding. 

The Ovens River at Eurobin may fall below the minor flood level (4.50 m) Saturday morning. The Ovens River at Rocky Point is currently at 4.28 m and steady, with minor flooding. The Ovens River at Rocky Point may peak near 4.30m Friday evening, with minor flooding. The river may remain above the minor flood level (3.20 m) during Saturday.
Stay informed - monitor your local conditions and remain alert.
What you should do: 
  • Be prepared to act if your situation changes.
  • Stay informed by listening to emergency broadcasters and monitoring warnings.
  • Floodwater is dangerous - never drive, walk or ride through floodwater.
  • Farmers should consider moving livestock and machinery to higher ground.
  • Floodwater is toxic - never play or swim in floodwater.
Impacts in your area: 
  • High river levels and potential flooding in areas close to rivers and streams.
  • Local roads may be closed and low bridges may be underwater.
  • Caravan parks and camping grounds may be flooded.
This message was issued by State Emergency Service.

The next update is expected by 04/07/2026 11:00 am or as the situation changes.

Use multiple sources to stay informed:
  • www.emergency.vic.gov.au
  • VicEmergency Hotline - freecall 1800 226 226
  • VicEmergency app
  • Facebook or Twitter (#vicfloods)
  • Tune in to ABC Local Radio, commercial and designated community radio stations, or Sky News TV.
Accessibility: 
The following services can help you, or someone you know, access information during an emergency. 
  • To access this information in other languages call the Translating and Interpreting Service on 131 450 (freecall) and ask them to call VicEmergency Hotline. 
  • If you are deaf, hard of hearing, or have a speech/communication impairment contact the National Relay Service and ask them to call the VicEmergency Hotline.

properties.name: Watch & Act
properties.webBody:
This Moderate Flood Warning is being issued for the Wonnangatta River.
  • This Watch and Act message replaces the Minor Flood Warning level issued at 07:00hrs 3rd July 2026. 
MODERATE FLOODING IS POSSIBLE AT WATERFORD FRIDAY AFTERNOON 

Moderate to heavy rainfall has been recorded across the Mitchell River catchment over the past few days,resulting in river level rises. Moderate flooding is possible along the Wonnangatta River at Waterford Friday afternoon.

Wonnangatta River: 

Latest River Heights:
Location
Wonnangatta River at Waterford 4.38 m Rising 12:23 pm Fri 03/07/26

Prepare for flooding now. Take actions to protect your life and property against potential hazards caused by flooding.


What you should do:
  • Farmers should move livestock and machinery to higher ground. 
  • Floodwater is dangerous - never enter floodwater.
  • Monitor weather forecasts and river levels. Go to www.bom.gov.au/vic/warnings/
  • Be prepared to move household items to higher ground, on top of tables, chairs, benches and beds. 
  • If you are camping close to a creek or river, you may be at risk of flooding and need to move to higher ground, away from waterways.
If you choose to leave:
  • Remember to take your pets, mobile phone, spare clothes and medications.
  • Travel to the home of family or friends who are in a safe location, away from flooding.
  • Turn off gas and electricity at your home or workplace.
  • Be aware of any road closures when you leave.
If you are travelling:
  • Do not enter flooded areas. Turn around a find a safe alternative route.
  • Be aware of road hazards including mud, debris and damaged roads or bridges.
  • Floodwater is dangerous - never drive, walk or ride through floodwater.
If you stay or if it is unsafe to leave:
  • Make sure you have enough food, drinking water, medications and pet food to survive for three to five days in case you become isolated.
  • Use plugs, sandbags or household items to block toilets, sinks and drains to stop sewerage backflow.
  • Lift it - move valuables and vital household items up on high surfaces, such as on top of tables, chairs, benches and beds.
Impacts in your area:
  • Heavy rainfall may lead to an increased risk of landslides – be aware of fallen earth, rocks, debris or trees.
  • You may lose services including power, water, sewerage and telephone due to flooding.
  • Main roads and bridges may be closed due to flooding.

This message was issued by State Emergency Service.

The next update is expected by 04/07/2026 02:00 pm or as the situation changes.

Use multiple sources to stay informed:
  • www.emergency.vic.gov.au
  • VicEmergency Hotline - freecall 1800 226 226
  • VicEmergency app
  • Facebook or Twitter (#vicfloods)
  • Tune in to ABC Local Radio, commercial and designated community radio stations, or Sky News TV.
Accessibility:
The following services can help you, or someone you know, access information during an emergency.
  • To access this information in other languages, call the Translating and Interpreting Service on 131 450 (freecall) and ask them to call VicEmergency Hotline.
  • If you are deaf, hard of hearing, or have a speech/communication impairment contact the National Relay Service and ask them to call the VicEmergency Hotline.

properties.name: Advice
properties.webBody:
This is a Final Minor Flood Warning for the Buckland River. 

Moderate rainfall since Monday has caused significant river level rises and areas of flooding across the Ovens and King River catchments. Showers are forecast to ease over the weekend.

River levels along the King River at Docker Road Bridge are currently peaking around the major flood level.

Moderate flooding is occurring along the Ovens River at Wangaratta.

Minor flooding is occurring along the Buffalo River downstream of Lake Buffalo, along the King River downstream of Lake William Hovell and along the Ovens River at Bright, Eurobin and Rocky Point.

Buckland River:

Flooding has eased along the Buckland River around Harris Lane.
The threat of flooding is reduced. Stay informed - monitor your local conditions and beware of hazards remaining in the warning area.
What you should do:
  • Be prepared to act if your situation changes.
  • Floodwater is dangerous - never enter floodwater.
If your home or business has been flooded:
  • Check for building damage before you enter.
  • Have all electrical and gas equipment professionally tested before use.
  • Clean and dry out the building as soon as possible.
Call Triple Zero (000) in a life threatening emergency. For emergency assistance from the SES call 132 500.
Impacts in your area: 
  • Damage to roads and buildings caused by flooding may still exist in your area. 
This message was issued by State Emergency Service.

This will be the last warning for this flood. 

Use multiple sources to stay informed:
  • www.emergency.vic.gov.au
  • VicEmergency Hotline - freecall 1800 226 226
  • VicEmergency app
  • Facebook or Twitter (#vicfloods)
  • Tune in to ABC Local Radio, commercial and designated community radio stations, or Sky News TV.
Accessibility:
The following services can help you, or someone you know, access information during an emergency.
  • To access this information in other languages call the Translating and Interpreting Service on 131 450 (freecall) and ask them to call VicEmergency Hotline.
  • If you are deaf, hard of hearing, or have a speech/communication impairment contact the National Relay Service and ask them to call the VicEmergency Hotline.

properties.name: Advice
properties.webBody:
This Minor Flood Warning is for the Seven Creeks to Euroa.

MINOR FLOODING OCCURRING AT EUROA AND POSSIBLE AT KIALLA WEST FROM SATURDAY MORNING

Since Monday, moderate rainfall has been recorded across the Seven and Castle Creeks catchment,resulting in creek level rises. Minor flooding is occurring along the Seven Creeks at Strathbogie and Euroa. Downstream at Kialla West minor flooding is possible from Saturday morning.

Seven Creeks to Euroa:

Minor flooding is occurring along the Seven Creeks at Strathbogie and Euroa.

The Seven Creeks at Euroa is currently at 3.23 m and steady, with minor flooding. The Seven Creeks at Euroa may remain above the minor flood level (2.50 m) until Saturday morning.

Stay informed - monitor your local conditions and remain alert.
What you should do: 
  • Farmers should consider moving livestock and machinery to higher ground. 
  • Download your Local Flood Guide to understand local flood impacts. www.ses.vic.gov.au/plan-and-stay-safe/flood-guides/strathbogie-shire-council
  • Review your emergency plan and check your emergency kit is fully stocked, if you have one. 
  • Monitor weather forecasts and river levels. Go to www.bom.gov.au/vic/warnings/
Impacts in your area: 
  • High river levels and potential flooding in areas close to rivers and streams.
  • Heavy rain over fire affected areas increases the risk of landslides. 

At the minor flood level of 2.5 metres:
On the Seven Creeks at Euroa, the Euroa Caravan Park, Rotary Park, and park area around the Memorial Oval and Bowls Club may start to be impacted by water.  

At the minor flood level 3.14 metres.
The area around the Memorial Ocal was inundated with water, closing the walking track. Minor flooding may occur on local roads. At this level in April 2020, Moglonemby Road was inundated with water.
This message was issued by State Emergency Service.

The next update is expected by 04/07/2026 03:00 pm or as the situation changes.

Use multiple sources to stay informed:
  • www.emergency.vic.gov.au
  • VicEmergency Hotline - freecall 1800 226 226
  • VicEmergency app
  • Facebook or Twitter (#vicfloods)
  • Tune in to ABC Local Radio, commercial and designated community radio stations, or Sky News TV.
Accessibility: 
The following services can help you, or someone you know, access information during an emergency. 
  • To access this information in other languages call the Translating and Interpreting Service on 131 450 (freecall) and ask them to call VicEmergency Hotline. 
  • If you are deaf, hard of hearing, or have a speech/communication impairment contact the National Relay Service and ask them to call the VicEmergency Hotline.

Goulburn River Lake Eildon to Seymour:
Minor flooding is occurring along the Goulburn River at Seymour.

The Goulburn River at Seymour is currently at 4.78 m and rising, with minor flooding. The Goulburn River at Seymour is expected to remain above the minor flood level (3.80 m) overnight Friday into Saturday. 


Stay informed - monitor your local conditions and remain alert.

properties.name: Advice
properties.webBody:
This Minor Flood Warning is being issued for the Ovens River at Bright.

MINOR FLOODING OCCURRING AT BRIGHT, EUROBIN, ROCKY POINT, DOWNSTREAM OF LAKE BUFFALO AND DOWNSTREAM OF LAKE WILLIAM HOVELL

Moderate rainfall since Monday has caused significant river level rises and areas of flooding across the Ovens and King River catchments. Showers are forecast to ease over the weekend.

River levels along the King River at Docker Road Bridge are currently peaking around the major flood level.

Moderate flooding is occurring along the Ovens River at Wangaratta.

Minor flooding is occurring along the Buffalo River downstream of Lake Buffalo, along the King River downstream of Lake William Hovell and along the Ovens River at Bright, Eurobin and Rocky Point.

Ovens River to Bright:

Minor flooding is occurring at Bright.

The Ovens River at Bright is currently at 3.01 m and steady, with minor flooding. The Ovens River at Bright
may fall below the minor flood level (3.00 m) Saturday morning.
Stay informed - monitor your local conditions and remain alert.
What you should do: 
  • Be prepared to act if your situation changes.
  • Stay informed by listening to emergency broadcasters and monitoring warnings.
  • Floodwater is dangerous - never drive, walk or ride through floodwater.
  • Farmers should consider moving livestock and machinery to higher ground.
  • Floodwater is toxic - never play or swim in floodwater.
Impacts in your area: 
  • High river levels and potential flooding in areas close to rivers and streams.
  • Local roads may be closed and low bridges may be underwater.
  • Caravan parks and camping grounds may be flooded.
This message was issued by State Emergency Service.

The next update is expected by 04/07/2026 11:00 am or as the situation changes.

Use multiple sources to stay informed:
  • www.emergency.vic.gov.au
  • VicEmergency Hotline - freecall 1800 226 226
  • VicEmergency app
  • Facebook or Twitter (#vicfloods)
  • Tune in to ABC Local Radio, commercial and designated community radio stations, or Sky News TV.
Accessibility: 
The following services can help you, or someone you know, access information during an emergency. 
  • To access this information in other languages call the Translating and Interpreting Service on 131 450 (freecall) and ask them to call VicEmergency Hotline. 
  • If you are deaf, hard of hearing, or have a speech/communication impairment contact the National Relay Service and ask them to call the VicEmergency Hotline.

properties.name: Watch & Act
properties.webBody:
This Moderate Flood Warning is being issued for the kiewa River at Bandiana.

MODERATE FLOODING LIKELY AT BANDIANA FROM FRIDAY EVENING.

MINOR FLOODING OCCURRING AT MONGANS BRIDGE AND KIEWA

Moderate to heavy rainfall across the Kiewa River catchment over the past few days has resulted insignificant river level rises along the Kiewa River. 

Minor flooding is occurring at Mongans Bridge and Kiewa, and moderate flooding is likely at Bandiana from Friday evening.

Kiewa River downstream of Mongans Bridge:

Moderate flooding is likely at Bandiana, and minor flooding is occurring at Kiewa. 

The Kiewa River Channel at Kiewa is currently at 3.41 m and steady, with minor flooding. The Kiewa River Channel at Kiewa is likely to remain above the minor flood level (3.30 m) through to early next week.

The Kiewa River at Bandiana is currently at 3.02 m and steady, with near moderate flooding. 

The Kiewa River at Bandiana is likely to reach around the moderate flood level (3.10 m) Friday evening.
Stay safe - avoid the flooded area. Be aware of potential hazards caused by flooding and never enter floodwater.
What you should do:
  • Floodwater is dangerous - never enter floodwater.
  • Farmers should move livestock and machinery to higher ground.
  • Rising floodwater can be dangerous. Be aware of water levels rising near you.
  • Floodwater is toxic - never play or swim in floodwater.
If you choose to leave:
  • Remember to take your pets, mobile phone, spare clothes and medications.
  • Travel to the home of family or friends who are in a safe location, away from flooding.
  • Turn off gas and electricity at your home or workplace.
  • Be aware of any road closures when you leave.
If you are travelling:
  • Do not enter flooded areas. Turn around a find a safe alternative route.
  • Be aware of road hazards including mud, debris and damaged roads or bridges.
  • Floodwater is dangerous - never drive, walk or ride through floodwater.
If you stay or if it is unsafe to leave:
  • Make sure you have enough food, drinking water, medications and pet food to survive for three to five days in case you become isolated.
  • Use plugs, sandbags or household items to block toilets, sinks and drains to stop sewerage backflow.
  • Lift it - move valuables and vital household items up on high surfaces, such as on top of tables, chairs, benches and beds.

Impacts in your area:

Impacts in your area:
  • Flooding above floor level of a single-story home is likely to occur in some locations.
  • Snakes, spiders and rats may try to find shelter in your home, even if raised.

This message was issued by State Emergency Service.

The next update is expected by 04/07/2026 11:30 am or as the situation changes.

Use multiple sources to stay informed:
  • www.emergency.vic.gov.au
  • VicEmergency Hotline - freecall 1800 226 226
  • VicEmergency app
  • Facebook or Twitter (#vicfloods)
  • Tune in to ABC Local Radio, commercial and designated community radio stations, or Sky News TV.
Accessibility:
The following services can help you, or someone you know, access information during an emergency.
  • To access this information in other languages, call the Translating and Interpreting Service on 131 450 (freecall) and ask them to call VicEmergency Hotline.
  • If you are deaf, hard of hearing, or have a speech/communication impairment contact the National Relay Service and ask them to call the VicEmergency Hotline.

properties.name: Advice
properties.webBody:
This Advice message is being issued for Scoresby.
  • There is a fire at a self storage facility on Koornang Road in Scoresby.
  • Smoke may be visible in the area.
  • Firefighters will remain on scene overnight.

What you should do: 
  • Keep the roads clear so emergency services can respond. 
  • If you are sensitive to smoke or you live with someone who is sensitive to smoke, you should close windows and doors. Turn off heating and cooling systems.
  • To prevent the smell of smoke entering your home or business, close windows and doors.  

This message was issued by Country Fire Authority.

The next update is expected by 04/07/2026 10:00 am or as the situation changes.

Use multiple sources to stay informed: 

  • www.emergency.vic.gov.au
  • VicEmergency Hotline - freecall 1800 226 226
  • VicEmergency app
  • Facebook or Twitter (#vicfires)
  • Tune in to ABC Local Radio, commercial and designated community radio stations, or Sky News TV.
Accessibility:
The following services can help you, or someone you know, access information during an emergency.
  • To access this information in other languages call the Translating and Interpreting Service on 131 450 (freecall) and ask them to call VicEmergency Hotline.
  • If you are deaf, hard of hearing, or have a speech/communication impairment contact the National Relay Service and ask them to call the VicEmergency Hotline.
